mnemonics.title: Key Environmental Laws记忆
mnemonics.content: WAPS: Water Act 1974, Air Act 1981, Protection Act 1986, Forest Act 1980 - chronological order of major Indian environmental legislation

acronyms.usage: Main umbrella legislation for environmental protection in India, enacted in 1986
National body coordinating with state boards for pollution control
Policy making producers responsible for product end-of-life management

acronyms.expansion: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change

visualTips.visualization: Pyramid: Top = International Agreements (Paris, Kyoto, Montreal) → Middle = Indian Constitution (Articles 48A, 51A) → Bottom = Specific Laws (EPA, Water Act, Air Act, Forest Act)
Two-tier structure: Central (CPCB) at top coordinating with State (SPCB) boards below, each having separate wings for air, water, and hazardous waste
Arrow flow: Production → Consumption → Collection → Segregation → Recycling → Disposal, with EPR adding circular arrow back to Producer

analogies.analogy: Just as traffic rules prevent accidents and ensure smooth flow on roads, environmental laws prevent pollution and ensure sustainable use of natural resources for the benefit of all.
Just as traffic police enforce rules and penalize violators on roads, pollution control boards monitor compliance and take action against industries violating environmental norms.
Just as a restaurant is responsible for cleaning up after customers, producers are made responsible for managing their products after consumers finish using them.
